comment.body:
if the momentum hits the upside 2 sigma for example, then the odds are very much priced higher for continued upward momentum. 
why? bcs plp tend to think that what happens now that will happen in the future too, our ability for abstract thinking is not that developped as many thinks..

comment.body: hi Alma, thank you for your wonderful thoughts. I'm a bit confused about the option expiration dynamics going forward. cem always mention the window of weakness starts wed before option expiration. Looking at Opex equations that you dropped. It seems like gamma dominates in the equation. So if there's a volatility event, and vol goes down as spot goes down, then spot is under the zero vanna line and the MM has negative vanna portfolio then they're forced to sell as spot goes down further? And as we approach the next option expiration, then vanna and vega shrinks, theta eats away at the options causing the net delta to be less negative, then they're forced to buy back the underlying as we approach the next option expiration? Is that the logic? Is this why the market bottoms around the 5th or 13th. 1 week before option expiration?

comment.body:
charm is pronunced near/at expos? - yes.
all the other statements: - nope.
Dealers have a book with net exposure. 
long otm put has positive charm,
long otm call has negative charm.

when you sum the book exposure you will get a negative or positive number.
We can roughly estimate the net exposure by watching the dealer skew, and also speed profile.
Speed is a good estimation for charm exposure: negative speed = positive charm, positive speed = negative charm.
bcs in negative speed you long otm puts and short otm calls,
in positive speed you short otm puts and long otm calls 
think about it
comment.raw_body:

when you sum the book exposure you will get a negative or positive number.
We can roughly estimate the net exposure by watching the dealer skew, and also speed profile.
Speed is a good estimation for charm exposure: negative speed = positive charm, positive speed = negative charm.
bcs in negative speed you long otm puts and short otm calls,
in positive speed you short otm puts and long otm calls

comment.body:
its not always windows of weakness, Cem simplifies it sometimes. 
remember what the formula showed about vanna vs vol relationship? higher vol = lower vanna effect. 
no vega effect on expo days as i showed.

negative vanna simply means that if vol rips, dealers buy bcs they are shorter delta.
and if vol declines, dealers sell bcs they are longer delta. 
this is why a negative speed is suppressive + net positive charm exposire (longer delta over time = sell by MMs)
forget theta here, charm decay matters. 

and don't try to derive everything from option greeks. thats jsut one part of the pic
